title: Consent Grant Abuse

dataSources: Entra audit logs (`Consent to application`, `Add OAuth2PermissionGrant`)
spl:
index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:audit"
| search "Consent" OR activityDisplayName="Add OAuth2PermissionGrant"
| spath path=targetResources{}
| mvexpand targetResources{} limit=500
| spath input=targetResources{} path=displayName
| table _time, initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName, displayName, activityDisplayName
| sort -_time
description: Users granting excessive delegated permissions to malicious OAuth apps is a common attack; monitoring consent events enables revocation.
value: Users granting excessive delegated permissions to malicious OAuth apps is a common attack; monitoring consent events enables revocation.
implementation: Ingest consent-related audit events. Alert on consent to apps with high privilege (`RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory`) or new publisher IDs. Integrate with admin consent workflow.
visualization: Table (consent events), Bar chart (apps by consent count), Pie chart (user vs admin consent).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Planned change windows, maintenance, approved automation, and known good service accounts; correlate with change tickets and identity team communication.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Entra audit logs (`Consent to application`, `Add OAuth2PermissionGrant`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest consent-related audit events. Alert on consent to apps with high privilege (`RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory`) or new publisher IDs. Integrate with admin consent workflow.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=azure sourcetype="azure:aad:audit"
| search "Consent" OR activityDisplayName="Add OAuth2PermissionGrant"
| spath path=targetResources{}
| mvexpand targetResources{} limit=500
| spath input=targetResources{} path=displayName
| table _time, initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName, displayName, activityDisplayName
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Consent Grant Abuse** ‚Äî Users granting excessive delegated permissions to malicious OAuth apps is a common attack; monitoring consent events enables revocation.

Documented **Data sources**: Entra audit logs (`Consent to application`, `Add OAuth2PermissionGrant`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: azure; **sourcetype**: azure:aad:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=azure, sourcetype="azure:aad:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
‚Ä¢ Expands multivalue fields with `mvexpand` ‚Äî use `limit=` to cap row explosion.
‚Ä¢ Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Consent Grant Abuse**): table _time, initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName, displayName, activityDisplayName
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Consent Grant Abuse** ‚Äî Users granting excessive delegated permissions to malicious OAuth apps is a common attack; monitoring consent events enables revocation.

Documented **Data sources**: Entra audit logs (`Consent to application`, `Add OAuth2PermissionGrant`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_microsoft-cloudservices`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with Entra ID sign-in and audit logs in the Microsoft Entra or Azure portal for the same users, resources, and time window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (consent events), Bar chart (apps by consent count), Pie chart (user vs admin consent).
grandmaExplanation: We use identity and sign-in data in Splunk so we can notice unusual logins, access changes, and privileged use while it still matters ‚Äî Consent Grant Abuse
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Change` datasets (for example `Change.All_Changes`, `Change.Auditing_Changes`, or `Change.Account_Management` depending on your mapping) once audit and identity events are CIM-tagged; otherwise `cimSpl` tstats may return no rows.
